concerns.text: The plan proposes a 'Paired t-test' for a design where 'No random assignment' exists (Observational Design). In an observational study, participants are not measured twice under different conditions; they are measured once in one condition. A paired t-test requires dependent samples (e.g., pre/post for the same subject). Applying a paired test to independent groups (nostalgia vs. control) is a fundamental statistical error that invalidates the p-values. The plan must specify an independent samples t-test or a mixed-effects model with random intercepts if repeated measures exist, but the current 'paired' assumption contradicts the 'observational' nature described.

The plan admits 'NO verified source found' for the core dataset (WCST + Age 65+ + Stimulus Type) and proposes using 'synthetic data' or 'manual curation'. A statistical analysis pipeline designed to test a specific hypothesis (nostalgia impact) cannot be validated or produce scientific results using synthetic data generated to match the schema. Synthetic data can only test code logic, not the empirical relationship. The validation target (the effect of nostalgia) is definitionally absent in synthetic data, making the primary analysis circular or vacuous. The plan must define a concrete, real-world data acquisition strategy or reframe the project as a 'methodological simulation' rather than an empirical study.

The plan assumes 'The study design is observational... so all findings will be framed as associational' but simultaneously plans to use 'nostalgia induction stimuli' which implies an experimental manipulation. If the data is observational, the 'stimulus_type' is a pre-existing label, not an induction. If it is an induction, it requires random assignment to be valid. The plan conflates 'exposure' (observational) with 'induction' (experimental). This ambiguity makes the choice of statistical test (paired vs. independent, regression vs. t-test) scientifically unsound until the data generation mechanism is clarified.
